pregame_model_read.pre_bet_thesis: Sanchez 6+ was too expensive, but 7+ at plus money aligned with the model median and provided superior risk/reward without chasing the 8+ ceiling.

what_worked: The model correctly rejected the juiced 6+ market and targeted the plus-money median at 7+.
Sanchez exceeded the 7+ threshold with 10 strikeouts across 7.0 innings.
The changeup was the engine: 45 pitches, 5 strikeouts, 11 whiffs, and 58% chase rate.
Overall chase rate reached 48%, confirming the pregame whiff sustainability thesis.
Overall CSW hit the green-zone threshold at 31%.
Sanchez carried full runway with 107 pitches and 26 batters faced.
risk_notes: Sinker contact was loud enough to matter: 95.4 average EV and 3 hard-hit balls on sinker BBE.
The win was not purely run-prevention safe; it was won because the changeup and slider generated enough chase and miss to overwhelm contact risk.
8+ would have hit, but the pregame no-8+ rule remains correct because 8+ required ceiling conversion and should stay live-confirmation only.
model_evaluation.patch_recommendation: Keep Sanchez plus-money median rule active. Prefer 7+ over juiced 6+ only when model median is 7, changeup whiff profile is intact, and opponent chase path is present. Do not auto-ladder to 8+ pregame unless live CSW/whiff indicators confirm ceiling.

notes: Clean plus-money median win. This entry reinforces that Sanchez's strikeout ceiling is driven by changeup chase/whiff conversion, while sinker contact risk should be monitored but not over-penalized when changeup form is strong.
